title: aegonychon arvense
intro: Aegonychon arvense, also known as Aegonychon or Wild Angelica, is a type of flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an expectorant to ease respiratory problems. Aegonychon arvense can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in traditional folk remedies for its purported healing properties.
